X-Last-Data-Change-At.description: Timestamp of last data modification (ISO 8601 UTC with millisecond precision)

X-Last-Data-Change-Rev.description: Revision counter for change detection. Compare values to detect data changes between requests.

X-RateLimit-Remaining.description: Remaining request capacity in current window

X-Sync-In-Progress.description: If `true`, response data is valid but background sync is running — more changes may follow shortly.

from.description: Start of the queried date range, inclusive (ISO 8601 UTC timestamp)

granularity.description: Time granularity of the usage entries

to.description: End of the queried date range, exclusive (ISO 8601 UTC timestamp)

total.description: Total API requests in the period

usage.description: Per-period request totals. Periods with zero usage are omitted.

from.description: Start of period, inclusive (ISO 8601 UTC timestamp, e.g., '2025-01-07T00:00:00Z')

to.description: End of period, exclusive (ISO 8601 UTC timestamp). This is the start of the next period, so the range is [from, to). For daily granularity, 'to' is 'from' + 1 day.

total.description: Total API requests in this period

accountType.description: Account type. Creatable: General (general purpose), Cash (physical cash), CurrentAccount (checking/current), SavingAccount (savings), Insurance (policy). Read-only (bank-sync/mobile only): CreditCard, Investment, Loan, Mortgage, Overdraft, Bonus (rewards/legacy)

balance.description: Computed account balance with credit card/overdraft corrections applied. Read-only. For non-credit accounts rawCurrentBalance equals currentBalance and credit fields are absent. For credit/overdraft accounts, balanceMode identifies the formula variant and formula shows the exact computation.

initialBalance.description: Account balance before any recorded transaction activity, in the account's original currency

isBankSync.description: Whether this account is connected to a bank via automatic sync (e.g. SaltEdge, Plaid). Read-only.

isInvestmentAccount.description: Whether this account contains investment portfolio data (stocks, funds, etc.). Read-only.

balanceMode.description: Computed balance mode derived from accountType, isBankSync, and credit card settings. Determines which formula variant is used. Read-only.

balanceModeFormula.description: Formula pattern showing both creditBalance and availableCredit variants for this balance mode. Read-only.
balanceModeFormula.example: creditBalance = -(initial + totalIncomes - totalExpenses); availableCredit = creditLimit + creditBalance

AgentHint.description: Structured hint for AI agents to understand API responses and take appropriate actions

action.description: Action data for instruction hints. Only present when severity=instruction. Contains url for fetching next page.

data.description: Context data specific to the hint type. Structure varies based on the 'type' field. See 'text' for human-readable explanation.

severity.description: Indicates the nature/importance of the hint

x-enum-descriptions.info: Neutral informational hint (e.g., all results returned, parameter inferred)
x-enum-descriptions.instruction: Agent should take action (e.g., fetch next page via action.url)
x-enum-descriptions.warning: Alert about potential issues (e.g., rate limit approaching)

x-enum-descriptions.pagination.has_more: More results available beyond this page. Use action.url to fetch next page.
x-enum-descriptions.rate_limit.warning: API rate limit is approaching. See data.remaining and data.reset_at for details.
x-enum-descriptions.result.empty: No records found. If filters were applied, data.appliedFilters shows them; otherwise, no records of this type exist yet.
x-enum-descriptions.result.partial_match: Some requested IDs were not found. Check data.missingIds for the list.

AgentHintsArray.description: Array of hints for AI agents. Only present when agentHints=true query parameter is set.

value.description: Amount value in decimal format (e.g., 1234.56 for one thousand two hundred thirty-four dollars and fifty-six cents). Not in cents.

clientErrors.description: Items that failed due to invalid input (client errors)

serverErrors.description: Items that failed due to internal errors (server errors)

categoryIds.description: Array of category IDs. System categories use deterministic IDs (e.g. "5c5c03e8-000a-8000-8000-000000000000"), custom categories use standard IDs.

closedDate.description: Date the budget was closed (YYYY-MM-DD). Present only when closed=true.

limitOverrides.description: Current+future period-specific limit overrides. Each entry: {limit (number), periodStart (YYYY-MM-DD), period (YYYY-MM / YYYY-Www / YYYY), createdAt (datetime, optional)}. Empty array means uniform limit across all periods.

pastLimitOverrides.description: Past period-specific limit overrides (before current period). Same shape as limitOverrides. Empty array means no past overrides.

type.description: Budget interval type. BUDGET_ALL is a read-only legacy type.

systemId.description: System category identifier (envelope slug). Present for system categories.

id.description: Category group slug — stable identifier for the root-level group (e.g. 'food_and_drinks', 'shopping', 'income')

error.description: Error message when rate is unavailable (other fields omitted except currencyCode)

ratio.description: Exchange rate used (1 unit of record currency = ratio units of target currency)

bankAccountNumber.description: Bank account number or IBAN (max 80 chars)

color.description: Universal color name. Randomly assigned if omitted.

categoryIds.description: Category IDs to track (system or custom)

type.description: Budget interval type. BUDGET_CUSTOM requires startDate and endDate. Type is immutable after creation.

color.description: Universal color name. Random color assigned if omitted.

accountId.description: Account ID to associate the record with

amount.description: Transaction amount. currencyCode is optional (defaults to account's currency). If provided, must match account's currency.

currencyCode.description: Currency code (e.g. USD). Optional — defaults to account's currency. Must match if provided.

value.description: Amount value (negative=expense, positive=income). Must not be zero.

categoryId.description: Category ID. System categories use deterministic IDs (e.g. '5c5c03e8-000a-8000-8000-000000000000'), custom categories use standard IDs.

recordDate.description: Record date in ISO 8601 format. Must not be more than 24 hours in the future.

results.description: Per-item results, one entry per input record

errorType.description: Error classification: client_error (bad input) or server_error (internal failure)

inputIndex.description: 0-based index in the input records array

color.description: Universal color name. Defaults to parent's color if omitted.

parentId.description: Parent base category ID. Use GET /categories to find available parent IDs.

errorType.description: Error classification: client_error (bad input) or server_error (internal failure)

error.description: Error message describing what went wrong

initialAmount.description: Initial amount in user's base currency

stateUpdatedAt.description: When the state was last changed

targetAmount.description: Target amount in user's base currency

limit.description: New spending limit (must be > 0). Mutually exclusive with setBaseline.

period.description: Starting period. Format: YYYY-MM (monthly), YYYY-Www (weekly), YYYY (yearly). Must be >= current period.

setBaseline.description: If true, reverts to the budget's baseline limit at this period. Mutually exclusive with limit.

limit.description: Number of items per page (applied limit)

nextOffset.description: Offset for next page (only present if more pages exist)

total.description: Total number of items matching the query filters. Only present when withTotal=true is set.

bankAccountNumber.description: Bank account number or IBAN (max 80 chars). Empty string clears the value.

initialBalance.description: Initial balance amount (e.g. 1000.00). Omit = no change.

account.description: Full account object on success (same shape as GET response)

errorType.description: Error classification: client_error (bad input) or server_error (internal failure)

accountIds.description: Account IDs to track. Array = replace all. Object {add, remove} = partial update.

categoryIds.description: Category IDs to track (system or custom). Array = replace all. Object {add, remove} = partial update.

closed.description: true to close (auto-sets closedDate to today UTC); false to reopen (clears closedDate)

endDate.description: End date. Only patchable on BUDGET_CUSTOM.

labelIds.description: Label IDs to filter transactions. Array = replace all. Object {add, remove} = partial update.

limitOverrides.description: Array of period-specific limit overrides (max 50). Replaces all current+future overrides. Not allowed on BUDGET_CUSTOM.

resetLimit.description: Clears ALL period overrides (past + future), sets uniform baseline limit. Works on all budget types. Can be combined with limitOverrides (resetLimit applies first).

startDate.description: Start date. Only patchable on BUDGET_CUSTOM.

category.description: Full category object on success (same shape as GET response)

errorType.description: Error classification: client_error (bad input) or server_error (internal failure)

name.description: New name (sets customName=true). Cannot be used together with resetName.

resetName.description: Set to true to reset name back to the system default. Only works on non-custom (base) categories. Sets customName=false. Cannot be used together with name.

errorType.description: Error classification: client_error (bad input) or server_error (internal failure)

label.description: Full label object on success (same shape as GET response)

$clear.description: Fields to clear/reset. Example: ["note", "place"]. Cannot be combined with an explicit value for the same field.

accountId.description: Move record to a different account (UUID). Both accounts must not be bank-synced and must have the same currency.

amount.description: Transaction amount. Omit = no change. Negative=expense, positive=income. Auto-updates recordType. Patching amount ALWAYS sets the record's currency to the account's currency, regardless of the record's original currency. If amount is omitted from the patch, the original currency is preserved.

currencyCode.description: Reserved for future use. Currently must match the account's currency if provided.

value.description: Amount value (negative=expense, positive=income). Must not be zero.

labelIds.description: Label IDs. Array = replace all (null=no change, []=clear all). Object {add, remove} = partial update (add to / remove from the existing set).

paymentType.description: Payment method. On paired transfer records (those with a linked mirror), amount/recordDate/recordState cannot be patched.

place.description: Location data. Omit=no change. Full replace with {address, latitude, longitude, name}. Use $clear to erase.

errorType.description: Error classification: client_error (bad input) or server_error (internal failure)

record.description: Full record object on success (same shape as GET response)

clientErrors.description: Items that failed due to invalid input (client errors)

serverErrors.description: Items that failed due to internal errors (server errors)

PlaceInput.description: Location data. Full replace on PATCH (no merge). Send empty object to erase, or use $clear with place.

RateLimitError.description: Returned when rate limit is exceeded. Check `X-RateLimit-Remaining` header before making requests to avoid hitting the limit.

example.error: Rate limit exceeded. Please try again later.

accountName.description: Account display name (read-only, derived from account)

amount.description: Transaction amount in its original currency.

category.description: Full category data embedded in response

counterParty.description: Counter party name (payee for expenses, payer for income)

paymentType.description: Payment method: cash, debit_card, credit_card, transfer, voucher, mobile_payment, web_payment

recordState.description: Transaction state: reconciled (verified against bank statement), cleared (processed by bank), uncleared (pending), void (cancelled/reversed), waitForAssign (awaiting categorization)

recordType.description: Transaction type: income (money received) or expense (money spent)

source.description: Client that created this record. android/ios = mobile app, web = web app, rest = REST API, mcp = MCP tool, backend = bank-sync or system-generated. Read-only.

transferRecord.description: Linked transfer record details (populated when this record is part of a transfer pair). Read-only.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the photo was created

temporaryUrl.description: Temporary signed URL of the photo (valid for limited time)

field.description: Field name used for the reference

ids.description: IDs of referencing entities (up to limit)

ReferencesResponse.description: Per-ID entry: either the references that point to the target entity, or an error object when the ID is absent or of the wrong type.

actualType.description: On 'type_mismatch', the entity type the ID actually exists under (account, category, record, or label).

error.description: Set when the ID could not be resolved: 'not found' (absent) or 'type_mismatch' (the ID exists but is a different entity type than requested).

message.description: Human-readable explanation accompanying 'type_mismatch' (names the actual type and notes the ID may be incorrect).

paymentCount.description: Number of payment occurrences tracked for this standing order

accountId.description: Account ID of the linked transfer record

amount.description: Amount of the linked transfer record

counterParty.description: Counter party of the linked transfer record

id.description: ID of the linked transfer record (other side of the transfer)

note.description: Note from the linked transfer record

UnauthorizedError.description: Returned when authentication fails (invalid/expired token or missing Authorization header)

ValidationError.description: Returned when request validation fails. Common errors: invalid filter format, limit must be between 1 and 200.

BearerAuth.description: JWT token obtained from authentication endpoint. The 'sub' claim in the JWT identifies the client_id.

info.description:
REST API for accessing Wallet financial data. Provides endpoints for financial records, accounts, budgets, and other financial entities for the Wallet application.
Query Filter Prefixes
• certain query parameters require inputs with filter prefixes
• Range filters: gt. gte. lt. lte. eq. — comparisons (>, ≥, <, ≤, =)
• Text filters:
    eq. — exact match
    contains. — substring (case-sensitive)
    contains-i. — substring (case-insensitive)
• see the API Reference for more details and examples
previous version: 1.2.2
info.title: Wallet API

get.description: Returns paginated list of financial accounts

parameters.description: Maximum number of items to return (1-200, default: 30)
parameters.name: limit

parameters.description: Number of items to skip (default: 0). Used for pagination.
parameters.name: offset

parameters.description: Enable AI agent hints in response. When true, includes structured hints to help AI agents understand the response and take follow-up actions.
parameters.name: agentHints

parameters.description: When true, the response includes a total count of all matching items (requires an additional database query). Default: false.
parameters.name: withTotal

parameters.description: Filter by ID. Supports comma-separated list for multiple IDs (max 30).
parameters.name: id

parameters.description: Filter by name. Requires text prefix.
parameters.name: name

parameters.description: Filter accounts by account type. Case-insensitive.
parameters.name: accountType

parameters.description: Filter by currency code (ISO 4217). Exact match, case-insensitive.
parameters.name: currencyCode

parameters.description: Filter by creation timestamp. Requires range prefix.
parameters.name: createdAt

parameters.description: Filter by last sync timestamp (when entity was last updated in the API database). Requires range prefix.
parameters.name: updatedAt

parameters.description: Filter by archived status. true=archived only, false=active only, omit for all.
parameters.name: archived

parameters.description: Filter items tracked by a specific budget.
parameters.name: budgetId

parameters.description: Sort field with +/- prefix for ASC/DESC. Allowed: createdAt, updatedAt. Example: -createdAt
parameters.name: sortBy

500.description: Internal server error. The request was valid but processing failed due to a temporary issue. Retry the request later.

patch.description: Batch update account fields. Maximum 10 accounts per request.

parameters.description: When false, omit the created/updated object body from each per-item result (results still carry id, success, and error). Default: true.
parameters.name: returnData

207.description: Mixed results — at least one item succeeded and at least one failed, or both client and server errors occurred. Check results array for per-item details.

400.description: All items failed with client errors (bad input, not found, validation). Body uses the same batch response schema — check results array for per-item error details.

429.description: Rate limit exceeded. Check `Retry-After` header for when to retry.

500.description: All items failed with server errors. Body uses the same batch response schema — check results array for per-item error details. Retry later.

500.description: Internal server error. The request was valid but processing failed due to a temporary issue. Retry the request later.

get.description:
▪️Returns REST API usage statistics for the authenticated client.
▪️All successful requests are counted (except this endpoint).
▪️Use period to specify the time range (e.g., 30days, 4weeks) and how it's grouped by a time period (daily, weekly, monthly).
▪️Time ranges of each period use half-open intervals: from is inclusive, to is exclusive.

parameters.description:
Time period for statistics.

**Formats:**
- `Xdays` → daily granularity (max `366days`)
- `Xweeks` → weekly granularity, ISO weeks, Monday start (max `53weeks`)
- `Xmonths` → monthly granularity (max `13months`)
parameters.name: period

429.description: Rate limit exceeded. Check `Retry-After` header for when to retry.

500.description: Internal server error. The request was valid but processing failed due to a temporary issue. Retry the request later.

get.description:
▪️Returns paginated list of financial transaction records.
▪️Use recordDate filter with range operators to scope results. If omitted, a default 3-month window is applied (see appliedRecordDateFilters in the response). Provide any single bound (e.g. gte.2020-01-01) to override.
▪️ID-based lookups (?id=) bypass the default window entirely.

parameters.description: Filter by ID. Supports comma-separated list for multiple IDs (max 30).
parameters.name: id

parameters.description: Filter by account ID (exact match). When omitted, returns records from all accounts. Use /accounts to find valid account IDs.
parameters.name: accountId

parameters.description: Filter by transaction date. Requires range prefix (e.g. gte.2024-01-01, lt.2024-04-01). If omitted, a default 3-month window is applied automatically. Provide any single bound to override the default. The actual applied filter is returned in appliedRecordDateFilters.
parameters.name: recordDate

parameters.description: Maximum number of items to return (1-200, default: 30)
parameters.name: limit

parameters.description: Number of items to skip (default: 0). Used for pagination.
parameters.name: offset

parameters.description: Enable AI agent hints in response. When true, includes structured hints to help AI agents understand the response and take follow-up actions.
parameters.name: agentHints

parameters.description: When true, the response includes a total count of all matching items (requires an additional database query). Default: false.
parameters.name: withTotal

parameters.description: Filter by category ID. Comma-separated for multiple values. Special value 'unknown' expands to IDs of 3 categories: Unknown income, Unknown expense, and Uncategorized. Max 10 values.
parameters.name: categoryId

parameters.description: Filter by label ID - returns records that have this label
parameters.name: labelId

parameters.description: Filter by note. Requires text prefix.
parameters.name: note

parameters.description: Filter records by counter party (payee/payer). Requires text prefix.
parameters.name: counterParty

parameters.description: Filter by transaction amount. Requires range prefix. Applied to amount.value (decimal format, e.g., 100.50).
parameters.name: amount

parameters.description: Filter by creation timestamp. Requires range prefix.
parameters.name: createdAt

parameters.description: Filter by last sync timestamp (when entity was last updated in the API database). Requires range prefix.
parameters.name: updatedAt

parameters.description: Filter by record type.
parameters.name: recordType

parameters.description: Filter by payment type.
parameters.name: paymentType

parameters.description: Filter by record state. Comma-separated for multiple values (e.g. recordState=reconciled,uncleared). Valid values: reconciled, cleared, uncleared.
parameters.name: recordState

parameters.description: Filter by record creation source. Comma-separated for multiple values (e.g. source=mcp,rest). Valid values: android, ios, web, rest, mcp, backend, missing.
parameters.name: source

parameters.description:
Sort results by field. Prefix with + for ascending, - for descending. Supported fields: recordDate, amount, createdAt, updatedAt. Default: -recordDate (newest first).

Examples:
- +recordDate (oldest first)
- -recordDate (newest first)
- +amount (smallest first)
- -amount (largest first)
- -createdAt (most recently created first)
- -updatedAt (most recently synced first)
parameters.name: sortBy

parameters.description: Convert record amounts to this currency. Accepts ISO-4217 code (e.g. EUR, CZK), 'base' (profile currency), or 'account' (record's account currency). When set, each record includes a convertedAmount field with value, ratio, currencyCode, and conversionPair.
parameters.name: convertTo

appliedRecordDateFilters.description: The actual recordDate filter applied to this request. Array of operator-prefixed strings (e.g. ["gte.2026-02-22T...", "lt.2026-05-23T..."]) when a date filter is active (including the implicit 3-month default). Null when no date filter is applied (id-based lookup).

429.description: Rate limit exceeded. Check `Retry-After` header for when to retry.

500.description: Internal server error. The request was valid but processing failed due to a temporary issue. Retry the request later.

patch.description: Batch update record fields (category, labels, note, amount, etc.). Maximum 10 records per request.

parameters.description: Validation mode. 'strict': validate all items first; if any client_error, return 400 with full results (no writes). Default: lenient (process valid items, skip invalid).
parameters.name: validation

parameters.description: When false, omit the created/updated object body from each per-item result (results still carry id, success, and error). Default: true.
parameters.name: returnData

207.description: Mixed results — at least one item succeeded and at least one failed, or both client and server errors occurred. Check results array for per-item details.

400.description: All items failed with client errors (bad input, not found, validation). Body uses the same batch response schema — check results array for per-item error details.

429.description: Rate limit exceeded. Check `Retry-After` header for when to retry.

500.description: All items failed with server errors. Body uses the same batch response schema — check results array for per-item error details. Retry later.

post.description: Create one or more transaction records. Maximum 50 records per request. By default each result includes the full created record; pass returnData=false to omit the record bodies (each result still carries inputIndex, id, success, and error).

parameters.description: When false, omit the created record body from each result (keeps inputIndex, id, success, error). Default: true.
parameters.name: returnData

207.description: Mixed results — at least one item succeeded and at least one failed, or both client and server errors occurred. Check results array for per-item details.

400.description: All items failed with client errors (bad input, not found, validation). Body uses the same batch response schema — check results array for per-item error details.

429.description: Rate limit exceeded. Check `Retry-After` header for when to retry.

500.description: All items failed with server errors. Body uses the same batch response schema — check results array for per-item error details. Retry later.

get.description: Returns paginated list of recurring payments. Default order: newest first (-createdAt).

parameters.description: Maximum number of items to return (1-200, default: 30)
parameters.name: limit

parameters.description: Number of items to skip (default: 0). Used for pagination.
parameters.name: offset

parameters.description: Enable AI agent hints in response. When true, includes structured hints to help AI agents understand the response and take follow-up actions.
parameters.name: agentHints

parameters.description: When true, the response includes a total count of all matching items (requires an additional database query). Default: false.
parameters.name: withTotal

parameters.description: Filter by ID. Supports comma-separated list for multiple IDs (max 30).
parameters.name: id

parameters.description: Filter by name. Requires text prefix.
parameters.name: name

parameters.description: Filter by currency code (ISO 4217). Exact match, case-insensitive.
parameters.name: currencyCode

parameters.description: Filter by label ID. Returns entities that include this label.
parameters.name: labelId

parameters.description: Filter by creation timestamp. Requires range prefix.
parameters.name: createdAt

parameters.description: Filter by last sync timestamp (when entity was last updated in the API database). Requires range prefix.
parameters.name: updatedAt

429.description: Rate limit exceeded. Check `Retry-After` header for when to retry.

500.description: Internal server error. The request was valid but processing failed due to a temporary issue. Retry the request later.

get.description: Returns paginated list of standing order items. Each item represents one occurrence (period) of a standing order and tracks paid/unpaid/dismissed status. Default order: newest first (-createdAt).

parameters.description: Maximum number of items to return (1-200, default: 30)
parameters.name: limit

parameters.description: Number of items to skip (default: 0). Used for pagination.
parameters.name: offset

parameters.description: Enable AI agent hints in response. When true, includes structured hints to help AI agents understand the response and take follow-up actions.
parameters.name: agentHints

parameters.description: When true, the response includes a total count of all matching items (requires an additional database query). Default: false.
parameters.name: withTotal

parameters.description: Filter items by standing order ID. Omit for cross-standing-order queries.
parameters.name: standingOrderId

parameters.description: Filter by original date. Supports range operators: eq., gt., gte., lt., lte. (e.g., gte.2024-01-01T00:00:00Z). Max 2 conditions.
parameters.name: originalDate

parameters.description: Filter by dismissed status (true/false).
parameters.name: dismissed

parameters.description: Filter items containing this record ID.
parameters.name: recordId

parameters.description: Filter by paid date. Supports range operators: eq., gt., gte., lt., lte. (e.g., gte.2024-01-01T00:00:00Z). Max 2 conditions.
parameters.name: paidDate

429.description: Rate limit exceeded. Check `Retry-After` header for when to retry.

500.description: Internal server error. The request was valid but processing failed due to a temporary issue. Retry the request later.

delete.description: Delete one or more entities by ID. The entity type is specified in the URL path. Maximum 10 IDs per request. For categories, records, accounts, and labels, use the references endpoint first to check if they are referenced by other entities. Budgets, standing-orders, and record-rules are not referenced by other entities, so no reference check is needed.

parameters.description: The entity type to delete
parameters.name: type

207.description: Mixed results — at least one item succeeded and at least one failed, or both client and server errors occurred. Check results array for per-item details.

400.description: All items failed with client errors (bad input, not found, validation). Body uses the same batch response schema — check results array for per-item error details.

429.description: Rate limit exceeded. Check `Retry-After` header for when to retry.

500.description: All items failed with server errors. Body uses the same batch response schema — check results array for per-item error details. Retry later.

get.description: Returns which entities reference the given entities. Accepts up to 10 comma-separated IDs via the id query parameter. Response is a map keyed by entity ID. Supported types: categories (returns records, standingOrders, budgets, recordRules), records (returns standingOrders via items.recordIds), accounts (returns records, standingOrders, budgets, recordRules, other), labels (returns records, standingOrders, budgets, recordRules, other).

parameters.description: The entity type to check references for. Plural forms are canonical; singular forms (category, record, account, label) are also accepted via normalization.
parameters.name: type

parameters.description: Comma-separated entity IDs to check references for (max 10)
parameters.name: id

200.description: Map of entity ID to references (or per-ID error object)

429.description: Rate limit exceeded. Check `Retry-After` header for when to retry.

500.description: Internal server error. The request was valid but processing failed due to a temporary issue. Retry the request later.
